denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 02/04/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100095028          Group #: GRP-1012
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-105665
Claim #: CLM-2026-188637
Date of Service (requested): 01/15/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners
 Dear Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/17/2026 for Additional psychotherapy visits beyond plan annual limit related to Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Additional psychotherapy visits beyond plan annual limit
Diagnosis (as submitted): Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria. Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM112 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM112 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Additional psychotherapy visits beyond plan annual limit is not considered evidence-based for Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1) under current medical policy. Published criteria do not support authorization. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with not evidence based. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials. APPEAL RIGHTS
If required information is not received within the applicable timeframe, the appeal may be administratively closed as withdrawn.
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100095028) and reference UM-2026-105665. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Additional psychotherapy visits beyond plan annual limit recommended as the next appropriate step. GAD-7 remained 16 after 16 sessions; prior auth supported weekly therapy for acute worsening in March 2026. Submitted documentation supports medical necessity for Additional psychotherapy visits beyond plan annual limit in the setting of Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1); the denial letter does not discuss those records or cite the objective findings in the file.
